free_text: Energy dependence of isospin mixing in
compound-statistical reactions
A counter telescope consisting of two
surface-barrier detectors.
Deduced energy dependence of isospin mixing;
tested independence hypothesis and spin-dependent
statistical theory.
OG
The 68Zn target consisted of self-supporting metallic
foils having an isotopic enrichment of 98.5% and a
surface density of 0.98. Energy loss measurements
performed with an 241Am alpha source served for the
thickness determinations.
Correction for small (1.5%) amounts of 64Zn, 66Zn,
and 67Zn in the 68Zn target.
No information on uncertainties.

The 65Cu target consisted of self-supporting metallic
foils having an isotopic enrichment of 99.6% and a
surface density of 0.83 mg/cm2. Energy loss
measurements performed with an 241Am alpha source
served for the thickness determinations.
No information on uncertainties.
The 65Cu target consisted of self-supporting metallic
foils having an isotopic enrichment of 99.6% and a
surface density of 0.83 mg/cm2. Energy loss

No information on uncertainties.
The 68Zn target consisted of self-supporting metallic
foils having an isotopic enrichment of 98.5% and a
surface density of 0.98. Energy loss measurements
performed with an 241Am alpha source served for the
thickness determinations.
Corrected by 2-7% for the contribution of the other
Zn isotopes present in the 68Zn target.
Uncertainty includes the standard
deviation in the least-squares fits to the angular
distributions,
estimated uncertainties in target
thicknesses (~3%),
current integration (~1%), and
solid angle determination (~2%).
Differential c/s with respect to angle
The 68Zn target consisted of self-supporting metallic
foils having an isotopic enrichment of 98.5% and a
surface density of 0.98. Energy loss measurements
performed with an 241Am alpha source served for the
thickness determinations.
Corrected by 2-7% for the contribution of the other
Zn isotopes present in the 68Zn target.
The error bars are an estimate of the
magnitude of the relative uncertainties in the data
comprising a given angular distribution and are based
on the agreement between occasional replicate
determinations, magnitude of impurity peaks,
agreement between Faraday cup and monitor detector,
and statistical uncertainties.
